leg: A — clone → Word, lists family (spec §8.3 step 1, slice 2)
method: Clone driven headlessly (--probe-out + --shot-evalfile): 5 paragraphs — p1 WC.Commands.run({cmd:'bullets'}); p2 run({cmd:'numbering'}); p3 WC.PM.cmd('toggleBulletListStyle','square'); p4+p5 selected together then WC.PM.cmd('applyListDefinition', <Decimal 1./1.1./1.1.1. pattern from commands.js ML_PATTERNS>), then p5 WC.PM.cmd('increaseListIndent'). Exported via WC.PM.exportDocxBytes -> wordAPI.saveBytes to tests/fixtures/.oracle-out-s2-lists.docx (untracked). The read-back through real Word could NOT be completed this session: the Word process's document-open machinery degraded to fully non-functional partway through the run (every open AppleEvent timed out even at 540 s; full diagnosis in 2026-06-06-slice2-verdicts.json -> sessionFindings). Word-side list evidence below comes from the same Word session reading basic-list.docx (the only file its recents/sandbox cache would still open) — proving the read-para-props verb and giving the real-Word expected property profile for the same numbering structures the fixture uses.
exportedXml: word/document.xml: each list paragraph carries <w:numPr><w:numId .../><w:ilvl .../></w:numPr> (numIds 4,5,6,7,7; ilvl 1 on p5). word/numbering.xml minted definitions verified structurally complete: every abstractNum has all 9 <w:lvl> levels with start/numFmt/lvlText/lvlJc and the Word hybridMultilevel indent convention <w:ind w:left='720|1440|...' w:hanging='360'/>; numId4 bullet lvlText '•' (Symbol font), numId5 decimal '%1.', numId6 bullet lvlText '▪', numId7 decimal '%1.'/'%1.%2.'. Two cosmetic nits recorded (not blockers): abstractNums minted by applyListDefinition lack <w:nsid> (abs 4 and 6), and the square-bullet lvl 0 carries an empty <w:rPr/> with no bullet font.

wordBaselineRead_basicList.what: Real-Word read-para-props profile of tests/fixtures/basic-list.docx — the same structures (hybridMultilevel numbering, numPr paragraphs) the fixture uses, read in THIS session with the same verbs
rows: bullet item: listType 'bullet', listLevelNumber 1, listString '-' (that file's lvlText), leftIndentPt 36, firstLineIndentPt -18, hangingPt 18
nested bullet: listType 'bullet', listLevelNumber 2, listString 'o', leftIndentPt 72, firstLineIndentPt -18
numbered items: listType 'simple numbering', listLevelNumber 1, listString '1.'/'2.'/'3.', leftIndentPt 36, firstLineIndentPt -18
nested numbered: listType 'simple numbering', listLevelNumber 2, listString 'a.', leftIndentPt 72
wordBaselineRead_basicList.note: Confirms the oracle reports listLevelNumber 1 vs 2 for top vs nested, negative firstLineIndentPt (hanging) on every list item, and lvlText-derived listString — exactly the expected profile for the slice-2 fixture's identical numbering shapes.

expected.check: p1 listType bullet + listString '•' (or Symbol PUA) + hanging metrics
expected.actual: read-para-props 2026-06-07 (relaunched Word, same fixture): listType 'bullet', listString '•' = literal U+2022 (Word PRESERVES the clone's exported lvlText — no coercion to Symbol PUA U+F0B7; the PUA reading applies only to Word's own default-bullet definitions), listLevelNumber 1, leftIndentPt 36, firstLineIndentPt -18, hangingPt 18
read-para-props 2026-06-07: listType 'simple numbering', listString '1.', listLevelNumber 1, leftIndentPt 36, firstLineIndentPt -18

expected.check: p3 square-glyph listString (DISCOVERY: '▪' vs Wingdings PUA)
expected.actual: RESOLVED 2026-06-07: Word reads listType 'bullet', listString '▪' = literal U+25AA — the clone's exported lvlText survives verbatim despite the empty <w:rPr/> (no bullet font); no PUA/Wingdings coercion. Level 1, leftIndentPt 36, firstLineIndentPt -18.

read-para-props 2026-06-07: BOTH report listTypeRaw 'list outline numbering' (first live sighting of that WdListType enum — multilevel definitions read as OUTLINE numbering, not 'simple numbering'); p4 listLevelNumber 1, listString '1.', leftIndentPt 36; p5 listLevelNumber 2, listString '1.1.', leftIndentPt 72; firstLineIndentPt -18 / hangingPt 18 on both

verdict: PASS — followUp closed 2026-06-07 on the relaunched Word session: all four expected rows verified against real Word (read-para-props on tests/fixtures/oracle-tmp-s2-lists.docx); clone-exported numbering round-trips with literal lvlText glyphs (U+2022, U+25AA) and the multilevel definition reads as 'list outline numbering'.
followUp: CLOSED 2026-06-07 — read-para-props on tests/fixtures/oracle-tmp-s2-lists.docx returned clean JSON for all 5 paragraphs (report archived at /tmp/s2-lists-followup.json during the session; verdicts inlined above). NOTE: the 2026-06-06 'open machinery died' diagnosis is refined by word-oracle quirk #25 — fresh-path opens complete LATE (minutes), they do not fail outright.
pidSafety: 2026-06-06: document count 0 before and after every attempt; the timed-out opens never materialized a document; Word never quit. 2026-06-07 followUp: a relaunch-restored copy of our own fixture was closed by exact basename saving no to reach baseline 0; count verified 0 after the read; Word never quit.
